Buyer is interested in AI-driven intake but immediately raises configuration-complexity concerns.
• On the same Feb 18 2026 call Pracha said about their current Ariba environment: 'The administration of the Ariba system is perceived as very difficult, requiring significant patience to support' and 'Maintaining Ariba system described as "really difficult" with manual configuration and multi-party support required.' He's clearly looking for AI-augmented simplification - but his framing is reactive (escape Ariba pain) not visionary (lead with autonomous procurement).
• High confidence: 2 valid Avoma meetings (siamcitycement.com domain), Feb 18 transcript with 83 notes including direct Pracha quotes on AI intake interest and concerns. Product scope (eProcurement;iSaaS;Merlin Intake;ANA) is AI-tilted. Stage = Formal Evaluation with shortlist confirmed. Boundary call - kept at 6 because buyer's AI interest came paired with concern about configuration, not unbridled enthusiasm.

competition.narrative: Primary competitor is SAP Ariba (incumbent). Buyer is in year 4 of 5-year Ariba contract with live 2026 renewal decision (Next_Step Nov 2025, discovery call 2026-02-18). Pracha stated on the 2026-02-18 call that they are 'exploring solutions in the market' ahead of the Ariba renewal and acknowledged Ariba's limitations (cumbersome, lacks AI intake automation, poor category workflow flexibility). However, Ariba has powerful incumbent advantages: (1) embedded SAP S/4HANA integration with their on-prem ERP, plus live integrations to blockchain ELT, DocuSign, Opentake, custom portal, and RPA bots—switching cost and IT risk are high; (2) IT familiarity and risk-aversion—group IT has not been engaged by Zycus, and without an IT champion the integration complexity may block a rip-and-replace; (3) inertia and lack of quantified ROI case—buyer has not articulated a compelling financial or strategic reason to switch, and the 65-day silence suggests they may renew with Ariba by default. Pracha said Zycus was 'shortlisted for RFP mid/late May' (Next_Step 2026-04-22), implying other vendors in the evaluation, but no RFP was issued and no other competitors were named. The competitive wedge is Merlin Intake AI automation (Ariba lacks it) plus Zycus category sourcing workflow flexibility, but Zycus has not demonstrated SAP integration credibility or built a quantified switching-cost ROI case. How we win: (1) secure group IT lead engagement and run a joint SAP integration deep-dive to prove low-risk migration; (2) co-build a quantified ROI case with Pracha and the Economic Buyer showing Merlin AI savings and category workflow efficiency gains vs Ariba renewal cost; (3) position Merlin Intake and ANA (Advanced Negotiation Analytics) as strategic AI capabilities Ariba cannot match, tied to buyer's digital transformation and cost-reduction mandate; (4) multi-thread to Supakit and the Thailand head of procurement to secure power sponsorship and break the operational-level stall.

Deal is dark and at high risk of being lost to Ariba incumbent renewal inertia.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and primary competitive threat. Buyer is in year 4 of 5-year Ariba contract with live 2026 renewal decision. Pracha articulated Ariba limitations (cumbersome, lacks AI intake automation, poor category workflow flexibility) on the 2026-02-18 discovery call, but Ariba has powerful advantages: embedded SAP/ERP integration, IT familiarity, and inertia. Buyer promised RFP mid/late May but never issued it, and 65-day silence suggests they may renew with Ariba by default. Zycus wedge is Merlin Intake AI automation and category sourcing workflow flexibility, but has not demonstrated SAP integration credibility or built a quantified switching-cost ROI case. Without Economic Buyer access, IT lead engagement, and a multi-threaded champion, Zycus cannot overcome incumbent advantage. How we win: (1) secure group IT lead and run SAP integration deep-dive to prove low-risk migration; (2) co-build quantified ROI case showing Merlin AI savings vs Ariba renewal cost; (3) multi-thread to Supakit and Thailand head of procurement (EB) to secure power sponsorship; (4) position Merlin Intake and ANA as strategic AI capabilities Ariba cannot match, tied to buyer's digital transformation mandate.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is partially strong but unvalidated and under-leveraged. Pracha expressed genuine excitement about Merlin Intake on 2026-02-18 discovery call: 'I'm excited to see how Merlin Intake can help us reduce manual procurement workload for indirect and services spend.' Products in scope include Merlin Intake and ANA (Advanced Negotiation Analytics), both AI-first. However, (1) no AIS score/status in Salesforce, so AI appetite is unquantified; (2) Merlin AI value has not been quantified in an ROI case—no baseline manual workload metrics, no projected cycle-time or headcount savings; (3) buyer's IT lead has not been engaged to validate AI/ML integration with their SAP/Ariba stack; (4) Ariba lacks Merlin-level AI intake automation, which is Zycus's strongest competitive wedge, but this wedge has not been formalized in a quantified business case or demonstrated in a deeper use-case session. The 65-day silence and stalled RFP suggest AI positioning has not created enough urgency or differentiation to overcome Ariba incumbent inertia. Under-positioned: yes—Merlin AI is the wedge but has not been weaponized with a quantified switching-cost ROI case or tied to a strategic business outcome (e.g., digital transformation mandate, cost reduction target).

value.detail: Incumbent advantage (SAP Ariba): buyer is in year 4 of 5-year Ariba contract with live 2026 renewal decision. Despite articulated pain (Ariba cumbersome, lacks AI intake, category workflow rigid), Ariba has embedded SAP integration, IT familiarity, and inertia. The 65-day silence and stalled RFP suggest buyer may renew with Ariba by default due to risk-aversion, IT reluctance to rip-and-replace, or lack of quantified business case proving Zycus ROI justifies switching cost. Without EB access, IT validation, and quantified ROI, Zycus cannot overcome incumbent advantage.
